insights.claim: The population-weighted U.S. average for poor physical health is 13.68%.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted mean across ZCTAs with estimates.

insights.claim: The most-deprived ADI tenth averages 7.0 points higher than the least-deprived tenth.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted ADI national-rank decile means; ecological, not causal.

insights.claim: Across ZCTAs, poor physical health is most associated with median household income (Spearman rho=-0.738, n=30,251).
insights.method_note: Spearman rank correlation; place-level association only.
Maximum ZCTA value; ties broken by population.

insights.claim: About 15.7 million people live in ZCTAs at or above the high-burden threshold (18.4%).
insights.method_note: Population in ZCTAs at or above the 90th percentile (18.4%).
